description: This set of micrographs illustrates κ-phase microstructures in 2Mn-8Al-0.2C (wt.%) low-density automotive steel. Subfigure (a) is an optical micrograph (20 μm scale bar) of the sample cooled from 1400 °C at 10 °C/s and quenched at 870 °C, showing κ-phase precipitated at the interface of austenite (γ) grains embedded in ferrite (α) matrix. Subfigure (b) is a 1.46 kX SEM micrograph (10 μm scale bar) of the same sample after deep etching, revealing the 3D morphology of needle-like κ-phase growing along the long axis of austenite. Subfigure (c) is a 2.00 kX SEM micrograph (10 μm scale bar) of the sample isothermally held at 870 °C for 15 min followed by deep etching, showing cocoon-shaped κ-phase remaining after full austenite decomposition. The figure confirms κ-phase preferentially precipitates at α/γ interfaces in low-Mn low-density steels, with distinct morphologies under different heat treatments, supporting the non-lamellar eutectoid decomposition mechanism of this alloy system.
